Analysis

Author

Sunduss Hamdan and Kaela Girod 

library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(mclust)
library(tidyclust)
library(factoextra)
library(broom)
library(sf)
library(h2o)

options(scipen = 999)

Load Data and Conduct EDA

Reading layer `nyc_final' from data source 
  `C:\Users\kkgir\Documents\Georgetown_Stuff\adv_data_science\final_project\data\nyc_final.shp' 
  using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 2327 features and 25 fields (with 3 geometries empty)
Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: -74.25563 ymin: 40.4961 xmax: -73.70036 ymax: 40.91771
Geodetic CRS:  NAD83
## Exploratory Data Analysis

nyc_final <- nyc_final |>
  mutate(borough = case_when(
    contyfp == "005" ~ "Bronx",
    contyfp == "047" ~ "Brooklyn",
    contyfp == "061" ~ "Manhattan",
    contyfp == "081" ~ "Queens",
    contyfp == "085" ~ "Staten Island"),
    borough = factor(borough))

## Poverty Rate by Borough
ggplot(nyc_final |> filter(!is.na(borough)), 
       aes(x = borough, y = pct_pvr, fill = borough)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 1.The Bronx Has the Highest and Most Unequal Poverty Rates Across\nNew York City (NYC) Boroughs",
    subtitle = "\nEach box shows the distribution of poverty rates across census tracts within each borough.\nThe middle line is the median tract, the box covers the middle 50%, and dots are outliers.",
    x = "Borough",
    y = "Poverty Rate (%)",
    caption = "\nSource: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, 2020-2024") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
nyc_final |>
  filter(!is.na(borough)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pct_bch)) +
  geom_histogram(color = "white", fill = "#8C7AA9") +
  labs(title = "The distribution of ownership of Bachelor's Degrees is relatively\nnot-skewed across Census Tracts",
       subtitle = "The center of the distribution seems to be around 25% owning a bachelor's degree",
       x = "% of Census Tract Population w/ Bachelor's Degree",
       y = "Frequency",
       caption = "\nSource: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, 2020-2024") +
  theme_minimal()
nyc_final |>
  filter(!is.na(borough)) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = pct_rnt)) +
  geom_sf() +
  labs(
    title = "The percentage of the population that are renters varies across\nNew York City Census Tracts",
    fill = "% Renting",
    caption = "\nSource: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, 2020-2024"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Exploration of Missingness

# drop geometry for further exploration
nyc_data <- nyc_final |>
  st_drop_geometry()

nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp, -borough) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "pct_missing") |>
  filter(pct_missing > 0) |>
  arrange(desc(pct_missing))
# A tibble: 17 × 2
   variable pct_missing
   <chr>          <dbl>

 2 chng_rn       0.0700
 3 mdn_rnt       0.0610

 5 mdn_ncm       0.0550
 6 mn_cmmt       0.0477
 7 pct_rnt       0.0413

10 chng_nm       0.0400

17 pct_fr_       0.0361
nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp, -borough) |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "pct_missing") |>
  filter(pct_missing > 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pct_missing)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "coral", color = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Figure 2. Distribution of Missing Values Across Variables",
       subtitle = "\nMost variables are missing in under 8% of tracts, with median home value variable\nthe exception at 16.5%",
       x = "% of values missing", 
       y = "Number of variables")
# check for highly correlated predictors

  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp, -borough) |>
  cor(use = "complete.obs") |>

# Look for the total number of incomplete cases. Don't just want to drop them
sum(!complete.cases(nyc_data))
[1] 451

Prepare Data for Modeling

# removing id variables for GLRM
nyc_model <- nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp)

nyc_ids <- nyc_data |>
  select(GEOID, NAME, contyfp)

Principle Component Analysis (PCA)

# replace infinite values with NAs
nyc_vars <- nyc_data |>
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.), NA, .)))

# create a pca recipe and impute missing values
pca_rec <- recipe(~ ., data = nyc_vars) |>
  add_role(GEOID, NAME, contyfp, borough, new_role = "id") |>
  step_rm(has_role("id")) |>
  step_impute_mean(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_nzv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_predictors(), num_comp = 7) 

# fit PCA
pca_rec_fit <- pca_rec %>%
  # run prep to prepare recipe
  prep()

# obtain summary metrics 
tidy(pca_rec_fit, number = 5, type = "variance") |>
  filter(terms == "cumulative percent variance") |>
  slice_min(value, n = 10)
# A tibble: 10 × 4
   terms                       value component id       
   <chr>                       <dbl>     <int> <chr>    
 1 cumulative percent variance  23.7         1 pca_bT8xg
 2 cumulative percent variance  38.2         2 pca_bT8xg
 3 cumulative percent variance  45.7         3 pca_bT8xg
 4 cumulative percent variance  51.7         4 pca_bT8xg
 5 cumulative percent variance  57.7         5 pca_bT8xg
 6 cumulative percent variance  62.7         6 pca_bT8xg
 7 cumulative percent variance  67.2         7 pca_bT8xg
 8 cumulative percent variance  71.5         8 pca_bT8xg
 9 cumulative percent variance  75.7         9 pca_bT8xg
10 cumulative percent variance  79.5        10 pca_bT8xg
# obtain loadings 
tidy(pca_rec_fit, number = 5, type = "coef") |>
  filter(component == "PC1") |>
  slice_max(order_by = abs(value), n = 7)
# A tibble: 7 × 4
  terms    value component id       
  <chr>    <dbl> <chr>     <chr>    
1 mdn_ncm  0.370 PC1       pca_bT8xg
2 pct_bch  0.349 PC1       pca_bT8xg
3 mdn_rnt  0.349 PC1       pca_bT8xg
4 pct_wht  0.335 PC1       pca_bT8xg
5 pct_pvr -0.277 PC1       pca_bT8xg
6 pct_hsp -0.248 PC1       pca_bT8xg
7 fd_bvr_  0.235 PC1       pca_bT8xg
#scree plot
tidy(pca_rec_fit, number = 5, type = "variance") |>
  filter(terms == "percent variance") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Scree Plot - Variance Explained by Each Principal Component",
    subtitle = "The elbow around component 7 indicates diminishing returns. Seven components are\nretained to explain 67.2% of total variance.",
    x = "Principal Component",
    y = "Percent of Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal()
# apply loadings to original data, keeping the first 7 components (small bend)
nyc_pca <- pca_rec_fit |>
  bake(new_data = nyc_vars) |>
  select(1:7)

nyc_pca
# A tibble: 2,327 × 7

# ℹ 2,317 more rows

K-Means Clustering

set.seed(4282026)

# set up 5-fold cross validation
nyc_cv <- vfold_cv(nyc_vars, v = 5)

# k-means spec with num_clusters = tune()
kmeans_spec <- k_means(
  num_clusters = tune()
  ) |>
  set_engine(
    "stats", 
    nstart = 100
    )

# workflow
kmeans_wflow <- workflow(
  preprocessor = pca_rec,
  spec = kmeans_spec
)

# tuning grid considering 1 to 10 clusters
clust_num_grid <- grid_regular(
  num_clusters(),
  levels = 10)

# tune the number of clusters
res <- tune_cluster(
  object = kmeans_wflow,
  resamples = nyc_cv,
  grid = clust_num_grid,
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, extract = identity),
  metrics = cluster_metric_set(sse_within_total, silhouette_avg))

# collect metrics
res_metrics <- res |>
  collect_metrics()

# elbow plot
res_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "sse_within_total") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(
    title = "Elbow Plot",
    x = "Number of Clusters",
    y = "Mean WSS over 5 Folds") +
  theme_minimal()
# silhouette plot
res_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "silhouette_avg") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(
    title = "Silhouette Plot",
    x = "Number of Clusters",
    y = "Avg Silhouette Width over 5 Folds") +
  theme_minimal()
# K means final model - Silhouette plot says 3, choosing 5 clusters based on elbow plot

# create a k-means specification with num_clusters = 5
kmeans_spec_final <- k_means(
  num_clusters = 5
) %>%
  set_engine(
    "stats",
    nstart = 100
  )

# create the final workflow
kmeans_wflow_final <- workflow(
  preprocessor = pca_rec,
  spec = kmeans_spec_final
)

# fit the final model
kmfinal_fit <- fit(
  kmeans_wflow_final,
  data = nyc_vars
)

# examine the cluster means 
tidy(kmfinal_fit)
# A tibble: 5 × 10
     PC1     PC2     PC3     PC4    PC5     PC6     PC7  size withinss cluster
   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <int>    <dbl> <fct>  
1 -0.816  0.0695  0.440  -0.707  -0.164 -0.769   0.0934   576    3604. 1      
2 -2.99  -1.92   -0.220   0.372  -0.494  0.462   0.0640   354    4930. 2      
3 -1.10   1.23    0.0595  0.751   1.55   0.417  -0.421    389    2341. 3      
4  1.04   1.11   -0.229  -0.0926 -0.631  0.162   0.167    702    3545. 4      
5  4.00  -2.02   -0.124   0.157   0.359  0.0110 -0.0964   306    3596. 5      
# create a dataframe that binds together the IDs, PCA, and cluster data
nyc_kmclusters <- bind_cols(
  nyc_final,
  kmfinal_fit |>
    extract_recipe() |>
    bake(nyc_vars),
  cluster = kmfinal_fit |>
    extract_cluster_assignment() |>
    pull(.cluster)
)

# view cluster means on key variables
nyc_kmclusters |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  group_by(cluster) |>
  summarise(across(c(mdn_ncm, mdn_rnt, pct_bch, pct_wht, pct_pvr, pct_hsp, fd_bvr_), \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
# A tibble: 5 × 8
  cluster   mdn_ncm mdn_rnt pct_bch pct_wht pct_pvr pct_hsp fd_bvr_
  <fct>       <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>
1 Cluster_1  73156.   1846.    19.1   21.9     18.0    40.3   3.85 
2 Cluster_2  40844.   1260.    12.7   11.9     36.2    51.2   1.53 
3 Cluster_3  85686.   1768.    18.7    7.24    14.2    14.2   0.812
4 Cluster_4  99400.   2051.    25.6   56.3     11.6    17.4   3.05 
5 Cluster_5 159107.   2953.    40.5   62.7     10.2    12.5  22.6

Visualizing the Clusters

# create a plot of the clusters with PC1 and PC2 as the x and y axis
nyc_kmclusters |>
  mutate(cluster = str_replace(cluster, "Cluster_", "")) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    mapping = aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = factor(cluster)),
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#e41a1c", "#984ea3", "#4daf4a",
                                "#377eb8", "#ff7f00")) +
  labs(
    title = "K-Means with K = 5 and PCA",
    x = "PC1 (23.7% of Variation)",
    y = "PC2 (14.5% of Variation)",
    color = "Cluster"
  ) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(text = NULL)
# map the clusters to NYC Census Tracts geographically
nyc_kmclusters |>
  mutate(cluster = str_replace(cluster, "Cluster_", "")) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = cluster)) +
  geom_sf() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#e41a1c", "#984ea3", "#4daf4a", 
                               "#377eb8", "#ff7f00")) +
  labs(title = "NYC Census Tracts by Cluster (K-Means)",
       fill = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal()

Generalized Low Rank Modeling (GLRM)

 Connection successful!

R is connected to the H2O cluster: 
    H2O cluster uptime:         3 days 3 hours 
    H2O cluster timezone:       America/New_York 
    H2O data parsing timezone:  UTC 
    H2O cluster version:        3.44.0.3 
    H2O cluster version age:    2 years, 4 months and 11 days 
    H2O cluster name:           H2O_started_from_R_kkgir_qjy117 
    H2O cluster total nodes:    1 
    H2O cluster total memory:   4.93 GB 
    H2O cluster total cores:    8 
    H2O cluster allowed cores:  8 
    H2O cluster healthy:        TRUE 
    H2O Connection ip:          localhost 
    H2O Connection port:        54321 
    H2O Connection proxy:       NA 
    H2O Internal Security:      FALSE 
    R Version:                  R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31 ucrt) 
# convert data to h2o object
nyc_model.h2o <- as.h2o(nyc_model)

# run basic GLRM
basic_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = nyc_model.h2o,
  k = 22, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  multi_loss = "Categorical",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 4282026
)

# get top level summary information on our model
## model converged at 881 iterations
summary(basic_glrm)
Model Details:

plot(basic_glrm)
archetypes <- as.data.frame(basic_glrm@model$archetypes) |>
  head(1) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "variables",
               values_to = "value")

archetypes |>
  filter(!str_detect(variables, "borough")) |>
  slice_max(order_by = abs(value), n = 7)

# scree plot
data.frame(
    PC  = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% seq_along(),
    PVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[2,] %>% unlist(),
    CVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[3,] %>% unlist()
) %>%
    gather(metric, variance_explained, -PC) %>%
    ggplot(aes(PC, variance_explained)) +
    geom_point() +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal()
t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes)[1:10, 1:5]
                          Arch1      Arch2       Arch3      Arch4       Arch5
boroughbronx         -5.7550183 -2.8661976 -1.52498161 -1.9520099  0.73907345

boroughstaten_island -3.3171815 -0.1919255 -1.05310968 -3.0612908 -1.54452763

unmply                0.1456290  0.2110959  0.45481884  0.6988936 -0.07072425
mdn_ncm              -0.6458470 -0.2325895  1.08382187 -1.0638140 -0.11047745
pct_bch              -0.7676811 -0.5839273  1.89600148  2.6353757 -0.25926909
mdn_rnt              -1.0455827  0.1647770 -0.17537995 -0.1090499 -0.68640549
# Based on the scree plot, choosing a rank of k = 5
k5_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = nyc_model.h2o,
  k = 5, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  multi_loss = "Categorical",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 4282026
)

nyc_glrm <- as.data.frame(h2o.getFrame(k5_glrm@model$representation_name))

# Reconstruct to see how well the model did
my_reconstruction <- h2o.reconstruct(k5_glrm, nyc_model.h2o, reverse_transform = TRUE)

my_reconstruction[1:5][1:5] %>% round(0)
  reconstr_pct_pvr reconstr_unmply_ reconstr_mdn_ncm reconstr_pct_bch
1               27               12            46336               13
2               39               17            14863                8
3               28               10            44865               11
4               30               13            42979               13
5               28               12            35681               10
6               40               17            14294                9
  reconstr_mdn_rnt
1             1383
2             1172
3             1371
4             1389
5             1224
6             1062

[2327 rows x 5 columns]

Gaussian Mixture Modeling (GMM)

set.seed(4282026)

# fit GMM
nyc_gmmfit <- Mclust(nyc_glrm)

#plot fitted model BIC and uncertainty
plot(nyc_gmmfit, what = "BIC")
# Best model has G = 8
glance(nyc_gmmfit)
# A tibble: 1 × 7
  model     G    BIC logLik    df hypvol  nobs
  <chr> <int>  <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <int>
1 VVV       8 21160. 11227.   167     NA  2327
augment(nyc_gmmfit)

tidy(nyc_gmmfit)

  component  size proportion mean.Arch1 mean.Arch2 mean.Arch3 mean.Arch4

# ℹ 1 more variable: mean.Arch5 <dbl>
nyc5_gmmfit <- Mclust(nyc_glrm, G =5)

# create a dataframe that binds together the IDs, archetypes, and cluster data

nyc_gmmclusters <- bind_cols(
  nyc_final,
  augment(nyc5_gmmfit))

# view cluster means on key variables
nyc_gmmclusters |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  group_by(.class) |>
  summarise(across(c(pct_rnt, prk_cnt, chng_rn, mdn_rnt, chng_nc, chng_pv, mdn_hm_), \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
# A tibble: 5 × 8
  .class pct_rnt prk_cnt chng_rn mdn_rnt chng_nc chng_pv  mdn_hm_
  <fct>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>
1 1         63.3   0.562    6.28   1729.    7.11   0.794  768843.
2 2         89.3   0.913   10.4    1351.   21.8   -3.25  1022545.
3 3         51.7   0.351    6.99   1782.    7.39   1.15   750750.
4 4         68.7   0.912    8.67   2594.    8.47   0.277 1249079.
5 5         63.2   0.822    6.17   1806.    4.44   0.965  767758.

Visualizing the Clusters

# create a plot of the clusters with Arch1 and Arch2 as the x and y axis
nyc_gmmclusters |>
  mutate(cluster = .class) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    mapping = aes(x = Arch1, y = Arch2, color = factor(cluster)),
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#e41a1c", "#984ea3", "#4daf4a", 
                               "#377eb8", "#ff7f00")) +
  labs(
    title = "Gaussian Mixture Modeling with G=8 and GLRM",
    x = "Arch1 (29.0% of Variation)",
    y = "Arch2 (20.4% of Variation)",
    color = "Cluster"
  ) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(text = NULL)
# map the clusters to NYC Census Tracts geographically
nyc_gmmclusters |>
  mutate(cluster = .class) |>
  ggplot(aes(fill = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_sf() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#e41a1c", "#984ea3", "#4daf4a", 
                               "#377eb8", "#ff7f00")) +
  labs(title = "NYC Census Tracts by Cluster (Gaussian Mixture Modeling)",
       fill = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal()
